free_text: Isobaric yields and radiochemistry of near-target
residues in the interaction of 12C and 16O with
103Rh at an incident energy of 400 MeV
Thin disks (15 mm diam., 79 mg/cm2 thick) of RhCl3xH2O
produced by pressing powder for both gamma and X-ray
Single stacks of metallic Rh foils consisted of a
single 5 um Ti monitor foil followed by several Rh
foils (nominally 32 mg/cm2 thick for 12C beam and
15.4 mg/cm thick for 16O beam) for gamma spectrometry
- coaxial intrinsic Ge gamma-ray detector with a
crystal diam. of 53 mm and 64 mm thickness.
- planar Ge X-ray detector with an active area of
200 mm2 and a thickness of 10 mm.
The metallic foils were counted in four times
(shortly after, several hours after, two weeks after,
RhCl3 disks were irradiated for (1) 1 hour to
(2) 5 hours to determine the longer-lived nuclides.
a small Faraday chamber mounted directly onto
an external beam line. Ti monitor foil also to served
as a beam monitor for a consistency check on the
current integration with the 48V production rate.
